results.possibleIdentity: John McGuire was a chemical engineer associated with Crawford & Russell Inc., a major engineering and construction firm headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut, which specialized in designing and building plants for the chemical, polymer, and petrochemical industries. McGuire was active in the late 1960s and 1970s, during which time he participated in collaborations through Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that paired technical experts with artists. His technical expertise spanned chemical engineering, mathematics, physics, and complex control systems including hydraulics, fluidics, and pneumatics. His background in plastics and polymers was particularly relevant to the industrial focus of Crawford & Russell, which was well-known for its work in polymerization and acrylic processes. He was a resident of the Stamford/New Canaan area in Connecticut, where many engineers from major firms like Crawford & Russell and IBM were based.
